sections.name: Consent Management
questions: Does your organization obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data?
Is your consent mechanism presented in clear, plain language?
Do you have separate consent mechanisms for different data processing activities?
Can users easily withdraw their consent?
options: Yes, with clear affirmative action required
Partially, but consent mechanisms need improvement
No, we rely on implicit consent
Yes, we use simple language and avoid legal jargon
No, our consent notices use technical/legal terminology
Yes, we obtain separate consent for each purpose
Partially, but some purposes are bundled
No, we use a single consent for all processing
Yes, through a simple, accessible process
Yes, but the process is somewhat complicated
No, withdrawal options are difficult to access

sections.name: Purpose Limitation
questions: Does your organization clearly document all purposes for which personal data is processed?
Is personal data used only for the specific purposes for which it was collected?
Do you have mechanisms to prevent data use beyond stated purposes?
options: Yes, all purposes are documented and reviewed regularly
No, purposes are not systematically documented
Yes, strictly limited to stated purposes
Yes, we have technical and policy controls

sections.name: Data Minimization
questions: Does your organization collect only data that is necessary for specified purposes?
Do you have processes to identify and remove redundant or excessive data?
options: Yes, we regularly review and minimize data collection
Partially, some unnecessary data may be collected
No, we collect data that may not be necessary

sections.name: Data Retention
questions: Does your organization have a documented data retention policy?
Are retention periods defined for different categories of personal data?
Do you have automated processes to delete or anonymize data after retention periods expire?
options: Yes, specific periods for each data category

sections.name: Data Subject Rights
questions: Does your organization have procedures to handle data subject access requests?
Can you provide data subjects with copies of their personal data in a structured, commonly used format?
Do you have processes to correct inaccurate personal data upon request?
Can you completely erase personal data upon valid request ('right to be forgotten')?

sections.name: Security Measures
questions: Does your organization implement appropriate technical security measures for personal data?
Do you conduct regular security assessments of systems processing personal data?
Do you have access controls limiting who can access personal data?
Is personal data encrypted at rest and in transit?
options: Yes, comprehensive measures following industry standards
Yes, role-based access with principle of least privilege
Partial encryption (either at rest or in transit)

sections.name: Data Breach Management
questions: Does your organization have a data breach response plan?
Can you detect data breaches in a timely manner?
Do you have procedures to notify authorities of data breaches within required timeframes?
Do you document all data breaches and remediation actions?

sections.name: Cross-Border Data Transfers
questions: Does your organization transfer personal data outside India?
If yes, do you ensure adequate protection for cross-border data transfers?
Do you maintain records of all cross-border data transfers?

sections.name: Data Protection Impact Assessments
questions: Do you conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing activities?
Are DPIA results incorporated into processing designs?

sections.name: Data Protection Officer and Governance
questions: Has your organization designated a Data Protection Officer or equivalent role?
Does your organization provide regular data protection training to staff?
Is compliance with data protection regulations regularly audited?

Consent Management.Partially, but consent mechanisms need i: Implement clearer consent mechanisms with explicit opt-in options
Consent Management.No, we rely on implicit consent: Replace implicit consent with explicit consent mechanisms
Consent Management.Partially, but needs simplification: Simplify consent language and avoid technical jargon
Consent Management.No, our consent notices use technical/le: Rewrite consent notices in plain, simple language
Consent Management.Partially, but some purposes are bundled: Separate consent for different data processing purposes
Consent Management.No, we use a single consent for all proc: Implement granular consent options for different processing activities
Consent Management.No, withdrawal options are difficult to: Make consent withdrawal options easily accessible

Purpose Limitation.Partially, some purposes are documented: Document all purposes for personal data processing
Purpose Limitation.No, purposes are not systematically docu: Create a comprehensive data processing register
Purpose Limitation.Mostly, with rare exceptions: Implement stricter controls to prevent purpose creep
Purpose Limitation.No, data is often repurposed: Establish clear purpose limitation policy and controls
Purpose Limitation.Partially, some controls exist: Strengthen controls to prevent data use beyond stated purposes
Purpose Limitation.No, limited controls exist: Implement technical and policy controls for purpose limitation

Data Minimization.Partially, some unnecessary data may be: Review data collection processes to minimize data collected
Data Minimization.No, we collect data that may not be nece: Conduct a data inventory and eliminate unnecessary collection
Data Minimization.No systematic process: Establish a systematic process for identifying redundant data

Data Retention.Partially, basic policy exists: Develop a comprehensive data retention policy
Data Retention.No documented policy: Create and implement a formal data retention policy
Data Retention.Partially, general periods defined: Define specific retention periods for each data category
Data Retention.No defined periods: Establish clear retention periods for all data categories
Data Retention.Partially automated with manual oversigh: Enhance automation of data deletion processes
Data Retention.No systematic process: Establish a systematic process for data deletion after retention periods

Data Subject Rights.Basic procedures exist: Enhance procedures for handling data subject requests
Data Subject Rights.No formal procedures: Establish formal procedures for data subject access requests
Data Subject Rights.Limited capability: Improve capability to provide data in structured formats
Data Subject Rights.No capability: Implement systems to export data in structured formats
Data Subject Rights.Basic process exists: Enhance processes for correcting inaccurate data
Data Subject Rights.Ad hoc handling: Formalize processes for handling correction requests
Data Subject Rights.No process: Establish processes for correcting personal data upon request
Data Subject Rights.Partially, in primary systems: Extend deletion capabilities to all systems
Data Subject Rights.Limited capability: Improve data erasure capabilities across systems
Data Subject Rights.No capability: Implement mechanisms for complete data erasure

Security Measures.Basic security measures: Enhance security measures with encryption and access controls
Security Measures.Minimal security measures: Implement comprehensive security based on ISO 27001
Security Measures.Rarely or never: Implement regular security assessments of data processing systems
Security Measures.Basic access controls: Implement role-based access with least privilege principle
Security Measures.Partial encryption (either at rest or in: Implement full encryption for data at rest and in transit
Security Measures.Limited or no encryption: Establish encryption standards for all personal data

Data Breach Management.Basic plan exists: Develop and test a comprehensive breach response plan
Data Breach Management.No formal plan: Create and implement a formal data breach response plan
Data Breach Management.Limited or no detection capabilities: Implement monitoring systems for timely breach detection
Data Breach Management.No formal procedures: Establish procedures for timely breach notifications

Cross-Border Data Transfers.Yes, to whitelisted countries only: Review adequacy decisions and transfer mechanisms
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Yes, including to non-whitelisted countr: Implement appropriate safeguards for all international transfers
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Basic measures: Enhance protection measures for cross-border transfers
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Limited or no measures: Implement adequate safeguards for international transfers
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Limited or no records: Establish comprehensive records of all data transfers

Data Protection Impact Assessments.Occasionally: Conduct DPIAs systematically for all high-risk processing
Data Protection Impact Assessments.Rarely or never: Implement DPIA framework for high-risk processing activities
Data Protection Impact Assessments.Sometimes: Ensure DPIA results are consistently incorporated into designs
Data Protection Impact Assessments.Rarely or never: Create processes to incorporate DPIA findings into system design

Data Protection Officer and Governance.Yes, as additional responsibility: Consider dedicated DPO role based on processing volume
Data Protection Officer and Governance.No designated role: Designate a Data Protection Officer or equivalent role
Data Protection Officer and Governance.Limited or no training: Implement regular data protection training for all staff
